title: Human Resources Management Course
description: The "Comprehensive Human Resource Management" course is specifically designed for business owners, CEOs, middle and senior managers of small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in Vietnam. The course provides comprehensive knowledge, skills, tools, and practical strategies for effective human resource management, cost optimization, and building a strong workforce. Students will be guided step-by-step from developing a human resource strategy aligned with business objectives to implementing modern tools such as HR management software, data applications, and process automation to improve business performance.
content_summary:
## Course Introduction

The "Human Resource Management" course provides a comprehensive roadmap for modern, people-centric HR management, tailored for small and medium-sized enterprises. The course enables learners to master strategies, tools, and practical processes from organizational structure design, recruitment, training, compensation, culture building, to the application of HRM technology and HR data analytics. The course stands out for its blend of up-to-date theory and detailed practical guidance, with a strong emphasis on applying advanced models, software, and management methods such as Agile, OKR, HR Analytics, and process automation. This course is suitable for CEOs, business owners, mid/senior managers, and HR departments seeking to build an effective HR system, optimize costs, and develop a sustainable team.

---

## Detailed Content by Chapter

### Course Overview

The opening chapter provides learners with an overview of the entire program, highlighting the role of HR management as the key to sustainable business development. The course consists of 8 modules, each focusing on a critical area: organizational structure design, comprehensive recruitment, training & development, compensation & benefits, corporate culture, and HR technology application. Learners will be equipped with up-to-date, practical knowledge, preparing them to confidently manage HR, build a cohesive work environment, and contribute to the long-term success of the business.

---

### Module 1: The Strategic Role of Modern HR Management

This module helps learners deeply understand the strategic role of HR management in modern businesses, especially in the context of digital transformation and talent competition. Learners will analyze the differences between traditional HR (mainly administrative, lacking strategic alignment) and modern HR (HR Strategic Partner) – where HR becomes a strategic partner, working alongside leadership, forecasting needs, and developing talent policies. The module presents the stages of HR development (administrative – professional – strategic partner), major challenges in Vietnam such as talent competition, digital transformation, flexible work, and legal updates. Learners will learn how to coordinate between CEO, HR, and middle management to build a sustainable HR system. Additionally, the module guides on lean HR organizational models for SMEs, core HR processes (recruitment, compensation, training, administration, performance management), culture building, cost optimization, and transparent compensation. Practical examples of HRM software application, online recruitment, remote interviews, and HR Analytics help learners clearly visualize the effectiveness and new trends in HR management.

---

### Module 2: Organizational Structure Design & HR Strategy

This module guides learners on how to build an organizational structure aligned with business goals, scale, strategy, and culture. Learners analyze the pros and cons of common structure models: functional, product/project-based, matrix, flat – along with the process of selecting, designing, testing, and adjusting the organizational model. The module emphasizes the necessity of aligning HR strategy with business strategy, ensuring goal synchronization, resource optimization, capability development, and corporate culture. Learners will learn the steps to build an effective HR strategy: identifying needs, assessing current status, planning recruitment/training/compensation, internal communication, measurement, and adjustment using tools like KPI, OKR, and Balanced Scorecard. Notably, the module delves into the Agile model in HR management, flexible thinking, Scrum and Kanban methods to help businesses quickly adapt, continuously improve, and enhance cross-functional collaboration. Real-world examples of organizational structure, HR strategies misaligned with business, Agile application in recruitment, and HR project management help learners understand practical implementation.

---

### Module 3: Comprehensive Recruitment & Onboarding

This module provides a 7-step sample recruitment process: identifying needs, job description, posting, CV screening, interviews (preliminary, official, technical, panel), offer, and onboarding handover. Learners will master interview techniques (STAR, behavioral, competency-based, situational, probing), objective candidate evaluation, using scorecards, managing profiles with Excel/ATS, and notes for online interviews. The module guides on building employer branding based on core values, benefits policies, development opportunities, and professional communication – with 4 steps to build a brand: defining EVP, storytelling, leveraging internal channels, measurement & adjustment. The onboarding section is detailed through 4 phases: pre-joining, first day, first week/month, after 1–3 months, with notes on role assignment, document preparation, creating a friendly environment, and promoting two-way interaction. Real-life examples of recruiting sales positions, building employer branding in retail, and onboarding processes help learners clearly visualize effective implementation.

---

### Module 4: Internal Training & Successor Leadership Development

This module delves into building an effective internal training system and maintaining continuous learning motivation for employees. Learners will learn how to identify training needs, plan, design programs (various formats: in-person, online, e-learning), evaluate – feedback, maintain, and improve content. The module emphasizes methods to foster learning motivation: building a learning culture, rewards, diverse training formats, linking to career progression. The successor leadership development section presents a 5-stage process: identifying needs, finding & evaluating potential staff, designing development paths (mentoring, coaching, rotation, challenge projects), practice & monitoring, evaluation & appointment. Learners are guided to build leadership competency frameworks, plan, budget, and measure effectiveness with quantitative & qualitative indicators. Real-world scenarios on sales skills training, developing new branch managers, and leadership training programs help learners apply them to their businesses.

---

### Module 5: Performance Management & Employee Evaluation

This module helps learners understand and build performance management systems (KPI, OKR), conduct fair, objective evaluations, and minimize bias. Learners will learn the process of setting up KPIs (selecting indicators, assigning weights, tracking, linking to rewards/penalties), OKRs (setting big goals, key results, cascading, regular check-ins), and how to combine KPI/OKR for different roles. The module guides on fair evaluation skills: data preparation, clear criteria, organizing two-way evaluation sessions, using 360-degree feedback, and self-assessment. Rewards and benefits linked to job results are detailed: reward forms based on KPI/OKR, supplementary benefits (certificates, gifts, leave, training), process of building reward funds, and transparent communication. Examples of sales department KPIs, marketing OKRs, employee evaluation scenarios, and supplementary benefits help learners recognize and apply them effectively in practice.

---

### Module 6: Compensation & Benefits

This module provides knowledge on building a competitive compensation structure aligned with business goals and resources. Learners will learn steps for market surveys, determining company position, choosing pay strategies (top pay, median, below market), defining salary bands, implementation, and adjustment. The module analyzes compensation components (base salary, allowances, KPI/OKR bonuses, commission, ESOP), rational arrangement between fixed and variable income, creating salary grades/levels, and flexible handling of special cases. The benefits and non-financial rewards section presents common programs (health insurance, flexible benefits, engagement environment, flexible work), cost optimization, partner negotiation, and measuring effectiveness. The module also covers legal, tax, social insurance, and labor law compliance notes. Examples of compensation structures, flexible benefits, healthcare, and cost balancing help learners implement suitable and effective policies.

---

### Module 7: Corporate Culture & Team Engagement

This module helps learners clearly understand the role and methods of building corporate culture, identifying and communicating core values, mission, and vision. Learners are guided through the process of building culture: leadership consultation, employee surveys, value selection, announcement, and integration into management processes. The module emphasizes the role of leadership as role models, realistic values, and thorough training for new employees. The team engagement section explains the reasons for engagement, types of activities (teambuilding, internal culture, charity, knowledge sharing, family activities), organization process (defining objectives, planning, communication, creating highlights, preserving memories), and notes for organizing (leadership participation, value connection, avoiding formality). Practical examples from Google, Netflix, Alibaba, e-commerce, logistics, manufacturing companies, and internal programs help learners deeply understand and effectively apply them in their businesses.

---

### Module 8: HR Technology, Data & Automation Application

This module equips learners with knowledge on applying HRM software, HR Analytics, and process automation to optimize time, increase transparency, and enhance competitiveness. Learners will learn the roles and main functions of HRM software (profile management, timekeeping, payroll, recruitment, training, performance, benefits), software selection – implementation process (identifying needs, comparing vendors, data preparation, installation, training, operation, evaluation). The HR Analytics section guides on collecting, standardizing, and analyzing HR data, using key metrics (attrition rate, recruitment cost, time to hire, training effectiveness, eNPS), benefits, and implementation notes. The module also presents the process of automating HR areas (timekeeping, payroll, leave processing, e-contract signing, profile management, recruitment, onboarding), implementation steps, security, legal compliance, and change management. Real-world examples of HRM automation, data analytics, and e-contract signing help learners recognize the value and implement successfully.

---

### Conclusion

The conclusion chapter summarizes the entire course content, emphasizing the roles and steps in building a modern HR system for businesses. Learners are reminded of the key modules: strategy, organizational structure, recruitment, training, evaluation, compensation, culture, technology – along with practical exercises to reinforce and apply the knowledge. The course is suitable for CEOs, business owners, managers, and HR departments, highlighting practical application and updates on technology trends and automation in modern HR management.

---

## Core Knowledge & Skills

- **Building HR strategies aligned with business goals:** Learners know how to analyze business strategy, identify HR needs, plan recruitment, training, development, and compensation in sync with company objectives.
- **Designing flexible organizational structures:** Proficient in selecting and designing suitable structure models (functional, product, matrix, flat), management hierarchy, delegation, testing, and adjustment.
- **Comprehensive recruitment process & employer branding:** Applying the 7-step recruitment process, using modern interview techniques (STAR, behavioral, competency, situational), building strong employer branding, and effective onboarding.
- **Building internal training systems & successor leadership development:** Identifying needs, planning, designing diverse training programs, maintaining learning motivation, developing leadership pipelines through mentoring, coaching, and real projects.
- **Performance management using KPI, OKR:** Setting up, measuring, tracking, and evaluating individual/department performance with KPI, OKR, organizing fair evaluations, using 360-degree feedback, linking rewards/benefits to job results.
- **Building competitive compensation & benefits structures:** Conducting market surveys, building salary bands, choosing suitable pay strategies, designing financial/non-financial benefits, optimizing costs, and ensuring legal compliance.
- **Building corporate culture & organizing engagement activities:** Identifying and communicating core values, mission, vision; organizing team engagement activities (teambuilding, charity, knowledge sharing), measuring cultural effectiveness.
- **Applying HRM technology, HR Analytics & process automation:** Selecting and implementing HRM software, analyzing HR data, automating processes (timekeeping, payroll, recruitment, onboarding), ensuring security, legality, and improving efficiency.

---

## Learning Outcomes

After the course, learners will be able to:

- Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with business goals and company scale.
- Design optimal organizational structures, allocate resources, delegate, and manage effectively.
- Apply comprehensive recruitment processes, use modern interview techniques, build strong employer branding, and organize effective onboarding.
- Establish and operate internal training systems, develop successor leadership programs, and maintain continuous learning motivation.
- Build performance management systems using KPI, OKR, organize fair and objective evaluations, link rewards/benefits to job results.
- Design competitive compensation structures, build flexible benefits programs, optimize costs, and ensure legal compliance.
- Build and maintain corporate culture, organize team engagement activities suitable for the business reality.
- Select and implement HRM software, apply HR Analytics, and automate processes to optimize time and enhance management effectiveness.
- Continuously evaluate and improve HR policies and processes based on data and practical feedback.

---

## Who Should Attend

This course is suitable for:

- CEOs, owners of small and medium-sized businesses, mid/senior managers, HR managers/deputies.
- Individuals responsible for or involved in building HR systems, team management, and business operations.
- Businesses seeking to optimize costs, improve HR management effectiveness, and build a professional, engaged work environment.
- No prior HR management experience required, but learners should have basic knowledge of business operations for effective learning.

---
